title: Memory limits should be enforced
description: A memory limit is a configuration that sets the maximum amount of memory that a container can use. It is part of the resource management functionality of Kubernetes, which allows for the control and allocation of computational resources to containers. When a memory limit is set for a container, Kubernetes ensures that the container does not exceed the specified limit.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Container is missing memory limits. Set resources.limits.memory to prevent memory resource issues.
